title: Field stack — KILROY PC core · AmmoOS desktop · Queen standalone browser
motto: Hardware untouched. KILROY boots the field (ZNetwork absorbed). AmmoOS loads on AMOURANTHRTX desktop. Queen is its own browser.
statement: KILROY is the PC core — network lane, loopback, defense. AmmoOS is the field OS with AMOURANTHRTX display technology for the desktop. Queen Browser is a standalone secured shell on :9481 — not a container for AmmoOS.

hardware_guard.motto: Witness BIOS, own field drivers in software — never brick hardware or kill host NIC manager prematurely

deprecated_layers.znetwork: Absorbed into KILROY network lane (lib/kilroy-core.sh) — not a separate boot layer
deprecated_layers.queen_canvas: AMOURANTHRTX display technology — owned by AmmoOS desktop, not a Queen sub-layer
deprecated_layers.ammoos_inside_queen: Retired v1 model — AmmoOS and Queen are sibling surfaces

layers_bottom_up.label: NEXUS C2
layers_bottom_up.motto: Command deck, gates, trust strike, panel API — absorbed into KILROY PC core at runtime

layers_bottom_up.label: KILROY PC core
layers_bottom_up.motto: Boots the machine, owns network lane (ex-ZNetwork), loopback 127.0.0.1, defensive perimeter

layers_bottom_up.label: AmmoOS
layers_bottom_up.motto: Field OS desktop — taskbar, programs, AMOURANTHRTX CANVAS backend; load via kilroy-load-os.sh or F9

layers_bottom_up.label: Queen Browser
layers_bottom_up.motto: Standalone rewritten browser — tabs, gates, GNU terminal; launched from desktop, not embedding AmmoOS
